chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions on how to link Rheonics sensor data (tags) with an Allen Bradley PLC within the Logix Designer software.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Linking tags with Allen Bradley PLC

Modified on: Sun, 15 Feb, 2026 at 12:53 AM

1. Go to “Controller Tag”, the data frame is available with all data that is sent from the SME. This is created once you add a new module.

Figure 1. Opening Controller Tags.

2. Rheonics sensors output the measurements which are referred as parameters. The data frame is expressed in Logix Designer as shown below once the Controller Tag is selected.
key_facts: The data frame containing all data from the SME becomes available once a new module is added.
Rheonics sensors output measurements, which are referred to as parameters.
The data frame representing sensor measurements is expressed in Logix Designer after the Controller Tag is selected.

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates the configuration of data arrays and function blocks within Logix Designer to process SME index values from an Ethernet/IP device, specifically for mapping viscosity, density, and temperature data.

Figure 2. Data arrays created in Logix Designer.

3. SME index values should appear in the PLC tag array as if it was an Ethernet/IP device and the function block CPS can be used to correctly line up the variables.

CPS Function Block Diagram (data frame matching):
  Block 1:
    CPS
    Source: VT2100.I.Data[2]
    Dest: VT2100_Viscosity
    Length: 4

  Block 2:
    CPS
    Source: VT2100.I.Data[15]
    Dest: VT2100_Density
    Length: 4

  Block 3:
    CPS
    Source: VT2100.I.Data[28]
    Dest: VT2100_Temp
    Length: 4

  Comparison Block 1:
    NEQ (Not Equal)
    Source A: VT2100_Viscosity
    Source B: 806.4487

  Comparison Block 2:
    NEQ (Not Equal)
    Source A: VT2100_Density
    Source B: 1.0

  Comparison Block 3:
    NEQ (Not Equal)
    Source A: VT2100_Temp
    Source B: 42.321

NOP (No Operation)

Figure 3. CPS function block. Used to match data frame.
key_facts: Data arrays, specifically RHEONICS.I.Data, are configured in Logix Designer for handling process data.
SME index values are intended to appear in the PLC tag array, mimicking data from an Ethernet/IP device.
The CPS function block is crucial for correctly aligning variables and matching data frames within the PLC program.
The Logix Designer interface displays Controller Tags with details including Name, current Value, Style (Decimal), Data Type (SINT), Class (Standard), and Description.
Raw data array elements (e.g., VT2100.I.Data[2], VT2100.I.Data[15], VT2100.I.Data[28]) are mapped by CPS blocks to named process variables like VT2100_Viscosity, VT2100_Density, and VT2100_Temp.
NEQ (Not Equal) function blocks are used to compare the mapped process variables (Viscosity, Density, Temperature) against specific reference values (806.4487, 1.0, and 42.321 respectively).
diagram_labels: Show: MotorControl, Recipe_Data, SCADA_Alarms, SCADA_RealTime_Data, SRV_PROBE_DATA
Figure 2. Data arrays created in Logix Designer.
Figure 3. CPS function block. Used to match data frame.

chunks.page_summary: This page details how Rheonics sensor data, including viscosity, density, and temperature, are presented in a PLC tag array and provides references for integration and troubleshooting related to Logix Designer and Ethernet/IP.
chunks.extracted_text:
